claim: For all integers n > 1, sigma(n) is at least n + 1, with equality exactly when n is prime.
predicate: n <= 1 or (sigma(n) >= n + 1 and (sigma(n) == n + 1) == is_prime(n))

claim: If n is composite, then sigma(n) is greater than n + 1.
predicate: is_prime(n) or sigma(n) > n + 1

claim: If n is prime, then sigma(n) equals n + 1.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or sigma(n) == n + 1

claim: If n is not a prime power, then sigma(n) is greater than n + 1.
predicate: is_prime(n) or (any((n % p == 0) for p in range(2, int(n**0.5) + 1) if is_prime(p))) or sigma(n) > n + 1
